content.problemStatement: Identify and correct the logic to print **true** if a number is even, else **false**.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "true" if N is even, otherwise print "false". Each answer is printed on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 1 is odd → false, 2 is even → true, 3 is odd → false, 4 is even → true, 5 is odd → false.
All three numbers (10, 20, 30) are even, so each yields true.
1 is odd → false, 2 is even → true.

solution.approach: The parity of an integer can be determined using the modulo operator. For any integer N, N % 2 equals 0 when N is even and 1 when N is odd. For each test case we read N, compute N % 2, and output "true" if the result is 0, otherwise "false". The algorithm runs in O(1) time per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few scalar variables are used.
